label: Philippines / Filipino
ladderNote: Filipino / Taglish from Inquirer, One Down, MentalHealthPH, and PH live-in coverage. Traditional courtship terms coexist with MU, syota, MOMOL, FUBU, DTR, and jowa.

levels.meaning: Nothing going on / single.
Attraction or romantic excitement. Kilig is the fluttery feeling around a crush or romantic moment.
Flirting, making romantic moves, or trying to be noticed.
Regular chatting or messaging with romantic potential, before a defined label.
Teasing or pairing off a possible couple; a classic early signal in Filipino courtship descriptions.
Very soft courtship: admiring from afar or joking/flirting before a direct move.
Courtship. The suitor is the manliligaw; the person being courted is the nililigawan.
Going out or seeing each other, without necessarily having an official relationship label.
Make out, make out lang: making out without attachment.
One-off casual sex.
Fuck buddy / friend with benefits; recurring casual sex without a committed relationship.
Short-time or informal romantic partner; often used before things are made official.
Mutual understanding: more than talking, not yet formal boyfriend/girlfriend. Also glossed in Bisaya as murag uyab, like a significant other.
An undefined romantic or sexual situation with no clear label.
Defining the relationship; moving from ambiguity to an agreed label.
Official significant other; jowa is the common gender-neutral slang substitute for boyfriend or girlfriend.
Partner / person you are in a relationship with; more adult or neutral than jowa.
Living together as a couple before or without marriage.
Engaged or moving through the Filipino pre-wedding family visit where marriage intentions are formally discussed.
Married / spouses.
Married with kids.
